Navigate your vehicle through busy intersections by timing your acceleration perfectly. Your car automatically stops at each crossing where traffic flows perpendicular to your path. Press SPACE to accelerate forward through gaps in the traffic. Collect coins for points and avoid collisions to survive. Each successful crossing increases the difficulty with faster and denser traffic. How many intersections can you conquer? SPACE: Accelerate vehicle forward through intersection ENTER: Start game from start screen ESC: Pause/unpause game R: Restart game (from game over screen) Testing basic movement mechanics and stopping at intersections. This validates that the player vehicle correctly stops at intersection edges and responds to acceleration input. Also tests collision detection with cross-traffic. The AI will wait at the first intersection for 2 seconds, then attempt to cross by pressing SPACE. It will try to cross during a gap in traffic. This tests whether the stopping mechanism works and if acceleration input is properly registered. The vehicle should stop at the intersection edge, accelerate when SPACE is pressed, and either successfully cross or collide with traffic. The test is successful if the vehicle responds to inputs correctly and collision detection works (game over on collision or successful crossing advances to next intersection). Testing progression through multiple intersections and difficulty scaling. This validates that the game properly increases difficulty after each successful crossing and that the player can complete several intersections. The AI will use a more sophisticated timing strategy, waiting for sufficiently large gaps in traffic before crossing. It will attempt to cross 5 intersections successfully, collecting any coins that appear along the way. The AI will calculate traffic gaps and time its acceleration accordingly. The vehicle should successfully cross multiple intersections, with visible increases in traffic density and speed. Score should increase with each crossing and coin collection. The test is successful if the player can cross at least 3 intersections, demonstrating that difficulty scaling works and the game is winnable with good timing. Testing coin collection and score mechanics. This validates that coins spawn correctly after crossings, can be collected by the player, and properly update the score. The AI will specifically target coins when they appear, adjusting timing to collect them while crossing. It will track score changes before and after coin collection to verify the scoring system works correctly. Coins should appear occasionally after successful crossings, be collectible by driving through them, and increase the score by the correct amount. The test is successful if at least 2 coins are collected and the score increases appropriately. Testing game over condition and restart functionality. This validates that collisions properly trigger game over and that the restart mechanism returns the game to a playable state. The AI will deliberately attempt to collide with traffic by accelerating at inappropriate times. After game over, it will wait and then press R to restart, followed by attempting a successful crossing to verify the game reset properly. A collision should immediately trigger GAME_OVER_LOSE state with appropriate UI. Pressing R should return to START screen, and pressing ENTER should allow gameplay to resume with reset score and difficulty. The test is successful if the full restart cycle works without errors. Testing pause functionality during active gameplay. This validates that ESC properly pauses and unpauses the game, and that game state is preserved during pause. The AI will start gameplay, cross one intersection, then press ESC to pause. It will wait for 1 second, then press ESC again to unpause and continue playing. This tests whether physics and game logic properly freeze and resume. The game should freeze all movement and physics when paused, display a pause overlay, and resume exactly where it left off when unpaused. Traffic positions and player state should be preserved. The test is successful if the game can be paused and unpaused without disrupting gameplay. Testing edge cases with rapid acceleration inputs. This validates that the game handles multiple quick SPACE presses correctly and doesn't allow exploits or cause errors. The AI will press SPACE multiple times in quick succession both while stopped at an intersection and while already moving. This tests input buffering and whether the acceleration mechanic can be exploited. Multiple SPACE presses should either apply a single acceleration or have a cooldown mechanism to prevent exploitation. The vehicle should not gain infinite speed or behave erratically. The test is successful if rapid inputs are handled gracefully without breaking physics or causing crashes. Testing traffic flow consistency and lane management. This validates that cross-traffic vehicles spawn correctly in their lanes, maintain proper speeds, and don't overlap or cause visual glitches. The AI will observe traffic patterns across multiple intersections without crossing, letting traffic flow for several seconds. It will monitor for gaps, consistent speeds, and proper lane discipline of cross-traffic vehicles. Traffic should flow smoothly across all lanes with consistent spacing based on difficulty level. Traffic Run

Navigate your vehicle through busy intersections by timing your acceleration perfectly. Your car automatically stops at each crossing where traffic flows perpendicular to your path. Press SPACE to accelerate forward through gaps in the traffic. Collect coins for points and avoid collisions to survive. Each successful crossing increases the difficulty with faster and denser traffic. How many intersections can you conquer?

Controls:
SPACE: Accelerate vehicle forward through intersection
ENTER: Start game from start screen
ESC: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart game (from game over screen)

TEST_1.expected_outcome: The vehicle should stop at the intersection edge, accelerate when SPACE is pressed, and either successfully cross or collide with traffic. The test is successful if the vehicle responds to inputs correctly and collision detection works (game over on collision or successful crossing advances to next intersection).
